tools.name: search_catalog
tools.description: Search the Zyla API Hub catalog for APIs by keyword. Searches the cached top-500 first; if not enough results, automatically searches ALL public APIs server-side. Returns matching APIs with their ID, name, description, category, and endpoints summary.

tools.name: get_api_info
tools.description: Get full details of a specific Zyla API by its numeric ID. Returns the API name, description, category, and all endpoints with their parameters and example values.

tools.name: call_api
tools.description: Call an API endpoint on Zyla API Hub. Requires the user's API key and a credit card on file. Billing is pay-per-successful-request: you only pay for 2xx responses. Each API has its own price per call (shown in get_api_info). There are no monthly subscriptions or minimums — charges appear on your cre

tools.name: browse_catalog
tools.description: Browse the full Zyla API Hub catalog with pagination. Use this to explore ALL public APIs beyond the top 500. Supports filtering by category and search keyword. Returns paginated results with total count and page info.

tools.name: list_categories
tools.description: List all available API categories on Zyla API Hub with the real count of public APIs in each. Use the category names returned here as the "category" parameter in browse_catalog to filter APIs by category. Example categories: Sports, Finance, Data, Science, etc.

tools.name: register
tools.description: Register a new Zyla API Hub account directly from the AI agent. Returns an API key AND a payment_url. The user MUST open the payment_url in their browser to add a credit card before making any API calls. Billing model: pay-per-successful-request with no monthly fees or minimums. Each API has its own

tools.name: account_status
tools.description: Check the current user's account status, billing, and usage. Returns plan info, API call count, whether a credit card is on file, and any failed/pending payments. Use this to diagnose why API calls are blocked (missing card or failed payment). Requires the user's API key in the Authorization header.

tools.name: get_payment_url
tools.description: Get a Stripe Checkout URL so the user can add or update their credit card. REQUIRED after registration and whenever a 402 error occurs. The user must open the returned URL in their browser to add/update their card. Billing is pay-per-successful-request: no subscriptions, no monthly fees, no minimums

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "account_status" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Check the current user's account status, billing, and usage. Returns plan info, API call count, whether a credit card is on file, and any failed/pending payment…
